title: Public playable Worker drain and terminal wait helper
summary: Expose a legal public root sim-worker/session helper so Web can advance and await authoritative WM-0150 playable UiDelta terminal or max-target state without debug polling, sim-core imports or UI-owned terminal authority.
acceptance: A public root @wuming-town/sim-worker BrowserSimulationWorkerSession helper or root helper lets Web advance the WM-0150 playable scenario and await an authoritative UiDeltaPayload.playable at or beyond an explicit target tick without importing @wuming-town/sim-core

A public root helper drains requested command ids until protocol-owned PlayableProjectionV1 terminal state or a caller-provided maxTargetTick is reached; terminal states are defined in the helper contract and derived only from PlayableProjectionV1

The helper uses the existing advancePlayableCommandScenarioToTick / PlayerCommandBatch path and existing UiDeltaPayload.playable delivery; no new top-level Worker message family is added unless ADR-0016 is superseded

apps/web consumes only @wuming-town/sim-worker and @wuming-town/sim-protocol public roots through apps/web/src/simulation-worker-session.ts; no app code imports sim-core, worker internals, parses summaries, polls debug payloads as gameplay state, or hardcodes wm0150.advance command ids

The helper posts the next explicit advance request only after receiving the prior authoritative playable UiDelta, keeps at most one in-flight target per helper invocation, returns posted MainToSimulationMessage objects, and exposes the final UiDelta/projection/result status
Destroy/cancel paths remove temporary subscriptions and do not leave hidden timers, background loops, global Worker/session singletons or pending promise state that can outlive the session
SetSpeed/Pause semantics remain unchanged; no autonomous browser clock loop, wall-clock simulation step, setInterval/requestAnimationFrame/setTimeout pump, or hidden real-time authority is introduced
Worker tests prove init -> accepted PrioritizeLampWork / QueueSimpleBuild -> public drain helper -> terminal or max-target PlayableProjectionV1 state for lamp/build/job/pawn/build/resource data

A real Chromium worker-smoke proves the public root browser session/helper path drains WM-0150 through projection tick progression and lamp/build completion without direct sim-core imports in the smoke module
Reliable CommandResult, SaveReady and fatal/error delivery remains separate from projection/drain delivery and is not dropped by larger or repeated UiDelta payloads
No product HUD, placement UI, renderer-pixi, ui-react, localization, shell-bootstrap implementation, public release, telemetry/account surface, or public save compatibility promise is added

Save/load stance is explicit: drain requests are active-session deterministic coordination only; in-progress playable command persistence remains unsupported unless a later versioned save task adds focused sections and migration/fixture evidence
The report states the exact public helper/session API names and the exact WM-0152 resume path for replacing debug hot-poll waits

history.details: Created to unblock WM-0152 after confirming WM-0157/WM-0158/WM-0159 provide command, projection and advance paths but no reviewed public awaitable drain/terminal helper for authoritative playable progression.
Implemented public root playable Worker wait/drain helpers, Web adapter aliases, terminal projection tests, Chromium worker-smoke coverage, and WM-0160 report; all required checks pass.

changes_requested: Blocker: lifecycle/message listener cleanup is not reliable for concurrent public wait/drain helpers. packages/sim-worker/src/browser-session.ts emits lifecycle/message listeners by iterating the live listener array, while packages/sim-worker/src/playable-drain.ts cleanup unsubscribes by splicing that same array during emit. With two pending wait/drain calls, session.destroy() rejects the first but skips the second, leaving a pending promise that can outlive the session. Fix by snapshotting listeners before emit or otherwise making unsubscribe-during-emit safe, and add focused coverage for multiple concurrent wait/drain helpers on destroy/shutdown and/or shared UiDelta delivery.
Addressed Keystone changes_requested: listener emit cleanup is safe under concurrent wait/drain; focused coverage and gates pass

verified: Verified WM-0160 after Keystone follow-up. Prior listener cleanup blocker is fixed by snapshotting ordinary, reliable, and lifecycle listener emits; concurrent wait and drain helpers settle on shared UiDelta, destroy, and shutdown. Public API remains root sim-worker with thin Web adapter aliases only; no product UI changes, new protocol message family, timer loop, singleton, sim-core import in apps/web, or deep import. Focused Vitest, Chromium worker-smoke, typecheck, boundaries, sim-run, validate-handoff, taskctl validate/status, and git diff --check pass.

Verified by systems-architect; integration gate passed in WM-0160 branch: typecheck, focused Vitest 26 tests, Chromium worker-smoke 17 tests, 240 tick deterministic sim-run, boundaries, validate-handoff, taskctl validate/status, git diff --check, pnpm quality 413 tests. Preparing main merge and push.

saveLoadStance: No public save compatibility is introduced. Drain requests are deterministic active-session coordination around authoritative Worker outputs only; in-progress playable command persistence remains unsupported until a separate versioned save/load task is approved.
forbiddenPaths: apps/web importing @wuming-town/sim-core, @wuming-town/sim-worker subpaths, or packages/*/src internals
apps/web hardcoding or parsing wm0150.advance command-id strings
apps/web or E2E parsing UiDelta summaries or debug payloads as gameplay terminal authority
product HUD, placement UI, renderer-pixi, ui-react, localization, shell-bootstrap, or web-shell E2E feature implementation beyond a thin simulation-worker-session adapter/test
UI-owned authoritative ticking, terminal classification, job state, pawn motion, resource counts, build progress, action availability or blocked reasons
hidden global Worker/session singleton, timer singleton, background loop, or wall-clock simulation authority
new autonomous browser clock loop via SetSpeed/Pause unless ADR-0016 is superseded with lifecycle/backpressure/replay semantics
new top-level Worker message family unless ADR-0016 is superseded with a stricter reason
new public save compatibility, migration or release promises
new runtime dependencies without a separate ADR
public release, Early Access, store, signing, telemetry, accounts, paid services or public feedback surfaces

review.summary: Verified WM-0160 after Keystone follow-up. Prior listener cleanup blocker is fixed by snapshotting ordinary, reliable, and lifecycle listener emits; concurrent wait and drain helpers settle on shared UiDelta, destroy, and shutdown. Public API remains root sim-worker with thin Web adapter aliases only; no product UI changes, new protocol message family, timer loop, singleton, sim-core import in apps/web, or deep import. Focused Vitest, Chromium worker-smoke, typecheck, boundaries, sim-run, validate-handoff, taskctl validate/status, and git diff --check pass.

integration.summary: Verified by systems-architect; integration gate passed in WM-0160 branch: typecheck, focused Vitest 26 tests, Chromium worker-smoke 17 tests, 240 tick deterministic sim-run, boundaries, validate-handoff, taskctl validate/status, git diff --check, pnpm quality 413 tests. Preparing main merge and push.
